CONTACT.ADDRESS: San Pedro No. 36, Col. La Cruz, Celaya, GTO 38028, Guanajuato, México

FORM.HEADING: Association Registration / Request Information:
FORM.DESCRIPTION: The main purpose of the association is to promote and highlight the industrial and professional fabric within the construction sector, companies and organizations working to define a new model of industrialized construction (off-site, prefabricated, sustainable and meeting circular economy criteria), fostering competitiveness and collaboration among associated members within the construction sector.

FOOTER.DIRECTION: San Pedro No. 36, Col. La Cruz, Celaya, GTO 38028, Guanajuato, México

NEWSLETTER.TITLE: Welcome to the Future of Construction
NEWSLETTER.SUBTITLE: Be part of the change. Connect. Transform. Build.
NEWSLETTER.BODY:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico is not just a business network: it is the hub where those who are redesigning the sector converge. Joining CCIM means entering an active community of innovation, where experience meets disruption and real opportunities for collaboration, visibility and growth open up.

Here, connections are forged with leading companies in the sector and a diverse range of SMEs and visionary professionals. We are rethinking every construction process toward industrialization, seeking to improve efficiency and sustainability. From new products and modular systems to algorithms that think faster than regulations, the ecosystem moves forward… and you can be part of it.

If you believe that industrialization, data and applied intelligence can transform the way we build, you are in the right place. If you are looking to add capabilities, share projects or scale your impact, join us today.

Pre-register, I will get in touch with you and send you detailed information to access our network.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Industrialization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Any actor, company, association or professional wishing to contribute to the process of improvement and modernization of the sector. Not only industrial companies, but also suppliers, SMEs or professionals who, through their experience, products or solutions, can contribute to the common goal.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: This layer of applications addresses a sector that has been resistant to digital technology. Equipping buildings, facilities or any construction with sensors to evolve the concept of home automation and bring it up to date with available technology.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Deep Digitalization
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Within the transformation process driven by CCIM, there is an emerging vision we call deep digitalization, summarized in the concept MAIA: Modular + Algorithms + Artificial Intelligence. This approach allows us to rethink the construction cycle from the ground up, connecting design, manufacturing, operations, commercialization and administration through intelligent data flows and adaptive modular structures. Drawing on advances in algorithmic design and procedural co-creation platforms, it demonstrates how not only processes are optimized, but the door is opened to a more evolutionary, connected and resilient form of construction.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Sustainability
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Applying the latest trends and solutions to create passive or energy-positive buildings. We must be champions of new green energies and energy consumption reduction. Construction may be the sector with the greatest potential for improvement in carbon footprint, circular economy and waste reduction.

SECTIONS.TITLE: Training
SECTIONS.DESCRIPTION: A new way of building requires a new way of teaching. Trades are the evolution of what we already know, but new ones are emerging. The knowledge of professionals must evolve with the market and new realities. That is why Training is a crucial issue in the sector's transformation. We want to partner with universities, vocational training centers, schools and companies to define and train the new professionals.

CO_FACTORY.TITLE: A New Way of Working
CO_FACTORY.DESCRIPTION:
Co-Factory is an industrial space. It provides a manufacturing infrastructure that allows access to projects that exceed a company's productive and technical capacity.

We are committed to creating a productive space, with teams specifically trained for industrialization, technical infrastructure and strategic support.

BANNER.TITLE: Co-Factory
BANNER.SUBTITLE: We drive the industrialization of construction

SECTION.TITLE:
Asset Investment
Operations Investment
SECTION.DESCRIPTION:
We foster a collaborative space with SMEs from CCIM, start-ups and sector professionals. Our public-private collaboration is fundamental to business development.

At CCIM we do not work only on short-term solutions; we have created a collaboration and debate tool to find the solutions that the sector needs as it undergoes transformation.

BANNER.TITLE: Co-Share
BANNER.SUBTITLE: The cluster organizes bimonthly events with partners in landmark venues, fostering networking.

SECTION.TITLE: Co-Share
SECTION.DESCRIPTION:
In that manifesto, the Federal Government of Mexico set a challenge:

'Create a BIM Working Group to help implement the culture, values, working methods, digital and electronic technologies to train the industry, public administration, research centers, professional associations and academia'.

Since then we have been building the Future, orienting our activities in that direction.

BANNER_INFO.TITLE: Industrialization Experiences
BANNER_INFO.DESCRIPTION: These events are linked to CCIM and its associates, offering collaboration opportunities with entities with potential projects, external guests sharing experiences aligned with CCIM's objectives, and visits to industrial spaces to understand sustainable processes, solutions and digitalization.

CO_SHARE.FOOTER: The gatherings offer a unique platform to learn, collaborate and explore advances in the industry.

SECTION.TITLE: Co-Mentor
SECTION.DESCRIPTION: We accompany companies that are undergoing an organizational transition, drawing on the experience of Cluster members who have driven disruptive change.

BANNER_INFO.DESCRIPTION: We offer meetings with two CCIM mentors to get to know the company: structure, product, market, channels and financial position. We carry out an internal analysis and a short, medium and long-term strategic plan. It also includes a LEAN Startup agreement to test new models and analyze impacts, as well as validation and adaptation of the model, including the possibility of financing.

CO_MENTOR.FOOTER: This mentorship program aims to be a practical boost for companies in transformation.

TEAM.TITLE: A Great Team
TEAM.DESCRIPTION:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico (CCIM) was born as a coordinated response from the country's leading public and private actors to foster a stable space for debate, action and real transformation of the construction sector. With the support of key entities such as the Chamber of Commerce of Mexico, the National Business Council and the Government of Mexico, CCIM has established itself as the meeting point where the master lines of the country's construction future are defined.

Since its founding, the cluster has oriented its activity toward the deep modernization of processes, with special attention to the integration of digital technologies, new organizational models and industrialized systems. This collective commitment to structural transformation — based on innovation, sustainability and collaboration — has made CCIM the benchmark space for all initiatives that want to rethink how the habitat of the future is designed, manufactured and managed.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Héctor Miranda Martínez
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in land transportation infrastructure construction with extensive experience in planning, execution and quality control of infrastructure projects in the public and industrial sectors.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Osvaldo Mireles
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in technology and sustainable development with specialization in Smart Cities, BIM, Blockchain, AI and the New Urban Agenda.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Jorge Moreno Vargas
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Expert in disruptive technologies, MIT Certified. 13 years leading innovation in smart cities, IoT and sustainable projects.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Teresita de Jesus Guerrero Escamilla
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Civil Engineer graduated from the Autonomous University of Querétaro in 2004. With more than two decades of experience, she has built a career defined by a commitment to engineering and the development of high-impact civil projects. Her active participation in institutional initiatives reflects her vocation for service and her ability to drive technical solutions that strengthen growth and innovation in the sector.

DIRECTORS.NAME: Ignasi Pérez Arnal
DIRECTORS.SEMBLANCE: Pioneer of BIM in Europe, co-founder of BIM Academy Spain and creator of the EUROPEAN BIM SUMMIT. He is the director of The Rebuild, a leading event in advanced construction and architecture in Spain. In addition, he leads training in BIM and digital transformation at prominent institutions, driving innovative projects in sustainability and urban planning.

TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.TITLE: Transformation of the Construction Sector
TRANSFORMATION_OF_THE_CONSTRUCTION_SECTO.DESCRIPTION:
The Industrialized Construction Cluster of Mexico (CCIM) was born from the union of professionals with solid and complementary backgrounds: from health applied to housing to architectural innovation, applied engineering, energy efficiency and industrial management; this strategic alliance allows us to shape a space for transformation.

We champion a new construction model based on coordination, digitalization and efficiency, using algorithmic methodologies alongside existing BIM and CAD tools, which allows the integration of each company's customized industrialized processes from conceptualization to key handover.

The world is digital and automated; procedural co-creation platforms that explore the intersection between systems and construction details, technology, design, sustainability, marketing and monitoring are tools that provide new perspectives on deep changes in the way we build.

With this combination of visions, we are consolidating ourselves as a key player in leading the paradigm shift in Mexico's construction sector, with an open, integrative outlook prepared to face the challenges of a world in continuous evolution.

CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.TITLE: Connection, Creating Collaborations
CONEXION_CREATING_COLLABORATIONS.DESCRIPTION:
We act as a connector within the industrialized construction ecosystem, generating spaces where companies, professionals, public administrations and knowledge centers can come together to share challenges, visions and strategies. Thanks to the diversity and experience of its members, an active trust network is created that facilitates the emergence of real collaborations.

These connections become tangible collaborations, shared projects and strategic alliances that allow complex challenges to be tackled from a collective approach. We build a bridge between innovation and action, fostering a collaborative culture in which different perspectives converge with a single purpose: to transform the sector toward a more efficient, sustainable construction industry prepared for the future.
